title: Fuyutsuki in 30
wikitext:
[]: Fuyutsuki in 3.0             Quote Post by bladerj » Sat Feb 02, 2013 5:51 am What is Fuyutsuki role anyway ? We are never told or shown what he actually does besides standing next to gendo and blabling stuff like he knows what is going on to feed the audience with exposition. What does he do at nerv anyway ? At least with Ritsuko we know she is a chief engeneer, fuy rank was something akin to second in chain of command,hence why gendo left him in charge while inspecting the dummy plug. But command of what ? they have katsuragi there for the pilots and operations.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat Feb 02, 2013 6:15 am I think that he also wants to see Yui again, (He might have crush on her like in EoE) and who knows, maybe he betrays Gendo and takes Yui to himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Feb 02, 2013 7:03 am We never know why Gendo dragged Fuyutsuki into Nerv, other than that he served as a confidant for Gendo and possibly as someone to direct operations when Gendo wasn't around (like in EoE). His role in 3.0 seems to be to do all the stuff Gendo doesn't need ReiQ for which he can't be bothered to do himself (operating the machinery which kills SEELE, for example).

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Sat Feb 02, 2013 8:07 am why do we need spoiler tags on subforum dedicated to spoiler ?

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat Feb 02, 2013 8:21 am bladerj wrote: why do we need spoiler tags on subforum dedicated to spoiler ?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 02, 2013 9:23 am For people reading by e-mail or RSS feed; and to blazon inter-thread links. Now, TOPIC, bitches.

[]:             Quote Post by Son of Lilin » Sat Feb 02, 2013 5:08 pm He's more of a plot device than anything. Then again, so are most of the characters in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Feb 03, 2013 1:17 am Outside of maybe the flashback episode, he served the exact same role in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Sun Feb 03, 2013 2:51 pm Oh Shit! I just realized something! It's Fuyutski that plays Shogi with Shinji and uses the metaphors! Fuyu is the real mastermind villian who is manipulating everyone! In Final he'll be revealed to be the big bad! Or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Sun Feb 03, 2013 5:05 pm I'm gonna make a guess and say that we'll have some (Read: barely any) light shed on Fuyutsuki in Rebuild once we see more of Yui. His character is entirely defined by his interactions with Gendo and Yui, and EOE gave some vague implications that he was secretly aiding in forwarding Yui's master plan all along. Even if she's not trying to pull a similar scheme in Rebuild (not likely), We're gonna see flashbacks with her and at least one of them will have Fuyutsuki in it and explain just what the hell he's actually there for. Fuyutsuki will remain essentially a prop, but his motivations will be revealed, if indirectly, simply because he's important to both of Shinji's parents.[/i]

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Tue Apr 30, 2013 4:15 pm REVIVE OLD THREAD FTW! I really, REALLY don't understand why Fuyutsuki is by Gendo's side? Why? Well, in 2.22, he states that, while on the moon base with Gendo, that it 'tears his heart to think that is our mother earth' and 'I rather the living chaos of man than this barren waste land of death' In 3.33, he seems very reluctant to follow Gendo, although he does it anyway for Yui's sake. My question is, if he doesn't like seeing the earth get fucked up so much...why is he still with Gendo in the first place? Well, he says in 3.0 for Yui's sake, but what exactly does that even mean? O.o. Did...did Yui wanted to start an Impact too? But that doesn't make any sense... Maybe it implies that he needs to look after Gendo?...If he his, he's kind of doing a bad job. Out of all the things in Q that I have been able to understand thanks to this community, I still can't understand Fuyustuki...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:26 pm My question is, if he doesn't like seeing the earth get fucked up so much...why is he still with Gendo in the first place? Well, he says in 3.0 for Yui's sake, but what exactly does that even mean? O.o. Did...did Yui wanted to start an Impact too? But that doesn't make any sense...

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:01 pm Yeah, what would make sense. I haven't seen the original series or watched EoE yet, so I didn't know about those implications. What was Fuyutsuki's relationship with Yui again in the original series? Didn't he secretly loved her?

[]:             Quote Post by Yokai » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:37 pm It's pretty interesting how Fuyutsuki plays a pretty normal father role in this movie, in some ways. The thing he told Shinji is something you'd expect a father to talk to his son about, and even makes a point to tell gendo that before doing so. In other words, I think his motive was merely to prove to "Yui" that he would have been a better father. Ironically, he's also a terrible father figure, because he gives him all of this information and doesn't imagine the repercussions from not following up and checking on him or giving him any sort of reassurance. It was merely a bitter act from a guy who's jealous of Gendo and his wonderful wife.

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Tue Apr 30, 2013 9:07 pm Yokai wrote: It's pretty interesting how Fuyutsuki plays a pretty normal father role in this movie, in some ways. The thing he told Shinji is something you'd expect a father to talk to his son about, and even makes a point to tell gendo that before doing so. In other words, I think his motive was merely to prove to "Yui" that he would have been a better father. Ironically, he's also a terrible father figure, because he gives him all of this information and doesn't imagine the repercussions from not following up and checking on him or giving him any sort of reassurance. It was merely a bitter act from a guy who's jealous of Gendo and his wonderful wife.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed May 01, 2013 5:27 am kboyrulez12345 wrote: Yeah, what would make sense. I haven't seen the original series or watched EoE yet, so I didn't know about those implications. What was Fuyutsuki's relationship with Yui again in the original series? Didn't he secretly loved her?

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Wed May 01, 2013 6:00 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I would highly recommend taking care of that, considering all the callbacks and inferences Rebuild makes to it! In sum, Fuyutsuki was Yui's professor, and he totally liked her but he's not a skeazebag so he remained a personal friend of her and Gendo. It seems that in the end, Yui trusted Fuyutsuki with her plans and he was the only person she seems to have told before fusing with Unit 01. In the original series at least, her goal was to intercept Instrumentality at the last minute and avert it so that humanity would keep living, since apparently Third Impact is "inevitable." But, y'know, Rebuild changes things so things could be different. Just giving you some context here.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 01, 2013 6:09 pm When I saw Fuyutsuki and Shinji in the same screen together, I had a fangasm moment, because it's one of those things I've always wanted: a scene of Shinji and Fuyutsuki interacting one-on-one. It never happened in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Wed May 01, 2013 6:17 pm Monk Ed wrote: When I saw Fuyutsuki and Shinji in the same screen together, I had a fangasm moment, because it's one of those things I've always wanted: a scene of Shinji and Fuyutsuki interacting one-on-one. It never happened in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat May 18, 2013 1:27 pm Something I wonder : was the infodump of Hell from Fuyutsuki's own volition, or did Gendo asked him to do it to further break Shinji and make him unstable enough to go for the spears even if Kaworu discovers that it's a trap? During the conversation between Kozo and Gendo in the ruined office of the later, Kozo said that he disagree about the fact that Gendo didn't showed to Shinji "how he(Gendo) lives", not that he don't tell him the truth about Rei or what happened to Yui 24 years ago. You could also think that Gendo asking Fuyutsuki to tell Shinji about Rei and Yui would be a strange move, since that would irremediably turn Shinji against him... except that they know that Kaworu showed him the world outside of the HQ, and had more than probably explained about HIP and the fact Gendo was working toward it, added to the fact that last time they met 14 years ago their relationship was pretty much dead, by that point Shinji is already against him, so giving him more reason to hate him wouldn't change anything for Gendo. Also maybe Fuyutsuki decided to told Shinji some of the truth by his own volition, after all he seemed to disagree with Gendo keeping Shnji into the dark, and about using EVA-13(implying using Shinji to pilot it). But what peeved me is that Fuyutsuki clearly saw Shinji's mental state when he proposed to play that game of shogi : the boy was muttering to himself, had lines below the eyes, his hands were shaking... clearly a bunch of revelations like the ones Kozo intended to tell to Shinji would have completely shattered his mind! Even if he thought that he was short on time because EVA-13 was almost finished, Gendo probably wouldn't have started the operation "two spears" until he was sure that whatever happens, EVA-13 retrieves the spears, meaning that even when Kaworu will notice that they are being tricked(and he's too knowledgeable to not see it) Shinji will do it. And Fuyutsuki's info-dump clearly was what sent Shinji beyond the edge, as the though that he at least saved Rei was the last thing Shinji clung to don't go mad. And don't forget that even if he seemed to disagree with Gendo using EVA-13, but rolled with it for Yui's sake, so in a way it was in his interest too that Shinji was too unstable to listen to reason once into Lilith's Chamber. On a side not, I'm sure that WILLE's intervention was not only planned by Gendo(he said it himself when they were in the Command Room similar to the Wunder's, and even sent Mark.09 to assist them in case of a WILLE attack) but actually triggered their intervention : Mark.09 could activate, fly with a giant jetpack to the Wunder and they couldn't detect it until it was attacking, yet when EVA-13 activated magically they detect it from hundreds of kilometers of distance? Please! Besides, if WILLE intervene, they will surely sent their two top dogs to intercept 13 and 09, Asuka and Mari, and the resulting fight will be a remake of one of Shinji's worst moments : having to fight against an EVA with Asuka inside, perfect to make him completely panic!
